response.justification: The candidate is a strong full-stack and web-platform engineer with genuine depth in TypeScript, Node.js, event-driven architecture, and high-throughput systems, but the role demands sustained, senior-level C++ expertise as its primary requirement. C++ appears only marginally on this resume—a brief six-month stint writing components for NI LabWindows and a bachelor-thesis wave-field-synthesis project—which is far below the 8–12 years of production C++ the position requires. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, message-broker-based industrial applications, MQTT, industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850), secure development per IEC 62443-4-1, or any industrial-automation domain experience. The skill gap is fundamental rather than peripheral.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to build high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) showing strong performance-engineering instincts
Hands-on event-driven and asynchronous programming experience (GISCollective, DLang async I/O, fibers) that is directionally relevant to the message-broker architecture the role requires
Consistent Agile/TDD/CI/CD discipline across multiple roles, plus mentoring and community teaching that aligns with the behavioral competencies listed
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent at a professional depth: two isolated data points totaling less than one year, with no evidence of STL mastery, concurrent systems design, or production C++ delivery
No industrial-automation domain knowledge and no exposure to any relevant industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 104, DNP 3.0, MQTT) or cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443)

Career trajectory is entirely web/cloud/full-stack; the seniority and specialization required for a C++-first embedded/industrial platform role represent a significant domain mismatch rather than a bridgeable gap
